Telehealth

zoom vs doxy.me therapists hipaa

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

zoom vs doxy.me therapists hipaa

You’ve been running sessions over FaceTime or the consumer Zoom plan because clients already know how to click the link. Then you realise—this isn’t HIPAA-compliant and you need a BAA yesterday. Now you’re stuck comparing Zoom for Healthcare vs Doxy.me, wondering which one won’t eat your whole budget or confuse clients.

This post walks you through the exact differences in pricing, compliance, and day-to-day use so you can pick one and get back to seeing clients.

Quick verdict:
Doxy.me Free gives you HIPAA-compliant 1:1 sessions at zero cost with no downloads; Zoom for Healthcare costs more but adds EHR hooks and group sessions. Pick the one that matches your cash-flow and feature list.

Zoom for Healthcare pricing

CurrencyAnnual cost per licence
USD$149.90–$240
GBP≈£120–£190
AUD≈A$230–A$370
CAD≈C$200–C$320

No monthly option on the healthcare tier—you pay for the full year up-front. If you need more than one provider licence, each gets billed at the same rate.

Zoom for Healthcare features

  • Business Associate Agreement (BAA) included on paid healthcare plans only
  • End-to-end encryption (must be enabled in settings)
  • HD video/audio with up to 500 participants
  • Waiting rooms, breakout rooms, session recording (stored under BAA)
  • Calendar and EHR integrations (Epic, SimplePractice, TherapyNotes, etc.)
  • Client must download the Zoom app on phones/tablets

Zoom for Healthcare pros

  • Clients already recognise the interface—fewer no-shows from tech panic
  • Video and audio quality stay crisp even on slower connections
  • Large meeting capacity if you run groups or webinars
  • Breakout rooms for couples or family work when you need separate sub-sessions
  • Cloud recording is BAA-compliant, so you can store session replays for supervision

Zoom for Healthcare cons

  • Only the paid healthcare plan is HIPAA-compliant; free or pro plans won’t sign a BAA
  • Mobile clients have to install the app—some workplace devices block downloads
  • Security toggles (waiting room, password, encryption) are buried in settings; one click missed = non-compliant session
  • Annual billing locks you in for the whole year even if caseload drops

Zoom for Healthcare best for

Solo therapists who already use Zoom socially and want EHR integration, group sessions, or HD recording for supervision notes.

Try ZOOM free →

Doxy.me pricing

CurrencyFree tierPaid tiers
USD$0$35–$50/month
GBP£0≈£28–£40/month
AUDA$0≈A$55–A$80/month
CADC$0≈C$48–C$70/month

Free tier is 1:1 only; if you need group sessions or scheduling, you jump to the monthly plan and can cancel any time.

Doxy.me features

  • BAA and HIPAA compliance on every plan, including free
  • Browser-based—no client downloads
  • End-to-end encryption enforced by default
  • Built-in waiting room, clinical intake forms, and simple text-chat
  • Limited third-party integrations (no native EHR sync)
  • Paid plans add appointment reminders, group calls, and white-labelling

Doxy.me pros

  • Zero-cost entry point for cash-strapped start-ups
  • Clients click a link and land straight in session—no app store friction
  • Automatic BAA—no extra settings to toggle
  • Waiting room and intake forms baked in, so you don’t pay for add-ons
  • Monthly billing lets you scale up or down with caseload

Doxy.me cons

  • Free tier caps you at 1:1 sessions—no couples or group work
  • Video quality drops more noticeably on poor connections than Zoom
  • Fewer integrations; if you live inside your EHR you’ll be doing manual copy-paste
  • Interface feels medical, which some counselling clients find cold

Doxy.me best for

Therapists who want a free, immediately HIPAA-compliant option and don’t need group sessions or deep EHR hooks.

Try DOXYME free →

Zoom vs Doxy.me head-to-head

FeatureZoom for HealthcareDoxy.me
HIPAA/BAAOnly on paid healthcare planOn every plan, including free
Cost entry~$150/year$0
Client downloadRequired on mobileNone—runs in browser
Group sessionsUp to 500Paid plans only (max 12)
EHR integrations20+ optionsVery few
EncryptionMust enableAlways enforced
RecordingBAA-compliant cloudPaid plans only

Who should choose which

Choose Doxy.me if:

  • You need HIPAA compliance today and cash-flow is tight
  • You see only individuals or couples who can join separately
  • You want the simplest client experience (no apps, no passwords)

Choose Zoom for Healthcare if:

  • You run groups, webinars, or multi-client workshops
  • You rely on EHR calendar sync to avoid double-bookings
  • You already use Zoom socially and don’t want to learn another tool

FAQ

Is Zoom free HIPAA-compliant for therapists?

No. Only the paid Zoom for Healthcare plan signs a BAA and meets HIPAA requirements.

Does Doxy.me free include a BAA?

Yes. The free tier automatically includes a Business Associate Agreement.

Can I do group therapy on Doxy.me free?

No. Group sessions require a paid Doxy.me plan; free is 1:1 only.

Which platform stores data in the US, and is that okay for UK therapists?

Both store data in US-based AWS/Azure regions. UK therapists should sign the provided Data Processing Agreement and register with the ICO.

Do clients need to create an account for either platform?

No. Clients click your room link and enter their name—no sign-up required.

Conclusion

If you need the cheapest, fastest route to compliant telehealth, start with Doxy.me Free—you’ll be seeing clients in minutes without spending a cent. When your practice grows into groups or you want EHR automation, upgrade to Zoom for Healthcare. Either way, pick one, get your BAA signed, and stop worrying about compliance.

Ready to get started?
Try ZOOM free → Try DOXYME free →
